translations.descricao: Acute sinusitis is an inflammation of the mucous membranes of the paranasal sinuses, often associated with viral or bacterial infections. Systemic antibiotic treatment is used in routine practice for exacerbated cases in adults and children. In children, oral therapy is recommended for symptomatic acute sinusitis. In adults, management includes oral antibiotics for confirmed infections.
translations.sus.indicacao: In the SUS, antibiotic treatment is indicated for suspected bacterial acute sinusitis in adults and children with persistent symptoms >10 days or worsening.
translations.sus.populacaoAlvo: Children and adults with symptoms of acute sinusitis, prioritizing cases at risk of complications.
translations.sociedadesMedicas.indicacao: Medical societies recommend antibiotics for acute sinusitis in adults and children only if symptoms >10 days, high fever, or worsening after initial improvement.
translations.epidemiologia.prevalencia: The prevalence of acute sinusitis is estimated at 10-15% of the annual population, with higher incidence in children. In Brazil, it affects about 11% of respiratory consultations.
translations.epidemiologia.incidencia: Global incidence of 0.5-2% per year, with seasonal peaks.
translations.epidemiologia.mortalidade: Low mortality (<0.1%), mainly in rare complications such as meningitis.
